citefrag 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Introduction to citefrag

Construct a citation panel object

Source: R/citation_panel.R
citation_panel.Rd

Creates a validated `citation_panel` object that bundles annual citation matrices, group assignments, and optional node size information. This object is the standard input to all analysis functions in citefrag.

Usage

citation_panel(matrices, groups, sizes = NULL)

Arguments

matrices

A named list of square citation matrices, one per period. List names are period identifiers (typically years as character strings). Each matrix must be numeric, square, with identical row and column names. The entry `M[i, j]` is interpreted as the number of citations from node `i` to node `j` in that period.

groups

A named character vector mapping node identifiers to group labels. The names must cover every node appearing in any matrix in `matrices`. Nodes not listed in `groups` will trigger an error.

sizes

Optional node size information for size-adjusted normalisations. Either a named numeric vector (time-invariant sizes, names matching the union of nodes) or a named list of named numeric vectors keyed by period identifiers (time-varying sizes, such as annual article counts). Default `NULL`.

Value

An object of class `citation_panel`: a list with elements `matrices`, `groups`, `sizes`, `periods`, `nodes`, `n_periods`, `n_nodes`, and `n_groups`.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Using bundled criminology data
data(criminology_panel)
print(criminology_panel)
} # }
